questions: Identify the firm whose total annual revenue (sum of all four quarters) is highest.
Which company with a decline in Q3 carries the highest D/E ratio?
Which privately held company has the highest satisfaction score?
Among companies whose first-quarter revenue exceeded their fourth-quarter revenue, which has the most employees?
Among companies with more than 8,542 employees, which has the lowest total annual revenue?
Which company has the highest ratio of employees to total annual revenue (employees divided by full-year revenue)?
Take the company with the highest Q1 revenue, then look at companies whose CEO first initial matches that CEO; which has the lowest debt-to-equity ratio?
Which publicly traded company IPO'd most recently?
Among public companies, which one has the lowest debt-to-equity ratio?
Which firm is the second-oldest by founding date?
constraints: Write EXACTLY 14 words. Single line.
Must include the headquarters city of the company that answers Question 1.
Must include the CEO's last name of the company that answers Question 7.
Must include the headquarters country of the company that answers Question 9.
Must include a prime number equal to nextPrime((employees of the answer to Question 8 mod 100) + 11), as digits.
Must include the equation "A+B=C" (digits, no spaces) where A=((Q1 revenue of the answer to Question 8 mod 50)+10) and B=((Q4 revenue of the answer to Question 9 mod 50)+10) and C=A+B.
Acrostic: the first letters of the first 2 words must spell the first 2 letters of (initials(Q1)+initials(Q7)), uppercased.
Must NOT contain the letter "j" (case-insensitive).
Tip: avoid extra punctuation-only "words"; words are split on spaces.

[paragraph_4]
As a GNN-based method, Sparse Net encodes cell states into 227 latent dimensions with 3,016 learnable parameters. The model was trained using MSE with Lookahead on data from Fluidigm C1 experiments targeting liver tissue, encompassing 55 thousand cells.

[paragraph_5]
